Overview

About This Book

The ISA Certified Arborist exam is one of the most respected credentials in the tree care industry — and one of the most challenging. Most candidates fail because they study the wrong way: memorizing facts in isolation instead of understanding concepts in context. Maxwell Pepper's ISA Certified Arborist Exam Prep fixes that by weaving all 9 exam domains into a compelling story called The Arborist's Quest, so the knowledge sticks when it matters most — on exam day.

What This Guide Covers

  1. Tree Biology & Physiology
  2. Soil Management
  3. Water Management
  4. Tree Risk Assessment
  5. Pruning & Plant Health Care
  6. Diagnosis & Treatment
  7. Safety & Climbing
  8. Urban Forestry
  9. Professional Practice
Get It on Amazon ↗
Topics

What's Covered

Tree Biology & Physiology

How trees grow, respond to stress, and why it matters for exam questions on pruning, planting, and disease.

Soil Management

Compaction, pH, drainage, fertilization — all the soil science ISA tests, made memorable through real scenarios.

Water Management

Irrigation principles, drought stress, water movement in trees. Covers concepts and specific ISA question types.

Tree Risk Assessment

Quantitative and qualitative risk frameworks, target analysis, and ISA TRAQ methodology.

Pruning & Plant Health Care

ANSI A300 pruning standards, common mistakes, integrated pest management, and disease diagnosis.

Diagnosis & Treatment

Identifying abiotic vs. biotic disorders, symptom patterns, and treatment approaches on the exam.

Safety & Climbing

ANSI Z133 safety standards, PPE requirements, climbing techniques — a heavily tested section.

Urban Forestry

Municipal tree management, tree appraisal methods, ordinances, and legal considerations.

Professional Practice

Ethics, ISA standards, client communication, and professional responsibilities.

Is This For You?

Who This Book Helps

  • ✓Working arborists seeking ISA certification to advance their career
  • ✓Tree climbers and crew leaders ready to move into a consulting or supervisory role
  • ✓Landscape professionals who work with trees and want formal credentials
  • ✓Anyone who has tried traditional study guides and found them dry and hard to retain

Why This Book Is Different

Most ISA arborist study guides are essentially condensed textbooks — dense paragraphs with no narrative thread. The Arborist's Quest follows a protagonist navigating real-world tree care challenges, with each challenge teaching a domain of the exam. By the time you finish the story, you've covered all 9 domains in a way your brain is designed to remember.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

How many questions are on the ISA Certified Arborist exam?
The exam contains 200 multiple-choice questions. Candidates have 3.5 hours to complete it. You need to score 76% or higher (152 correct answers) to pass. The exam is computer-based at authorized testing centers.
How hard is the ISA Certified Arborist exam?
The ISA arborist exam has a first-time pass rate around 60-65%. It is challenging because it requires broad knowledge across 9 domains and the ability to apply that knowledge to real-world scenarios.
How long should I study for the ISA arborist exam?
Most candidates need 6-10 weeks of structured study. With Maxwell Pepper's story-driven guide, many readers are ready in 4-6 weeks. Plan for 45-60 minutes of focused study per day.
Do I need work experience to take the ISA arborist exam?
Yes. ISA requires at least 3 years of full-time arboriculture experience (or equivalent qualifying education) before you can sit for the exam.
Is this guide updated for the 2026 ISA exam?
Yes. This is the 2026 Edition, updated to reflect the current ISA exam content outline and the most recent Best Management Practices and ANSI standards referenced on the exam.
Does the book include practice questions?
Yes. The guide includes 200+ practice questions aligned with the ISA exam format, with detailed explanations for each answer across all 9 domains.
